English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Douglas V. Cannon papers
Cannon, Douglas V., -1974.
3014
2 cubic feet. 2 cubic feet.
[1958-1973?] Correspondence, printed materials, publications, and manuscripts. Pamphlets, speeches, correspondence, articles, and books, largely authored by Douglas Cannon, relating to land economics and the International Fraternity of Lambda Alpha, including the Golden Gate and the Los Angeles chapters. Collection material in English 2022901
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
December 2014
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, December 2014
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, April 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Urban economist.
Douglas V. Cannon held many positions with federal agencies, including the Federal Housing Administration and the United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration. He served as International President for the International Fraternity of Lambda Alpha from 1967 to 1969, and was a trustee of the Lambda Alpha Foundation. Founder, International Fraternity of Lambda Alpha, Golden Gate Chapter.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Pamphlets, speeches, correspondence, articles, and books, largely authored by Douglas Cannon, relating to land economics and the International Fraternity of Lambda Alpha, including the Golden Gate and the Los Angeles chapters.
NOTES
Part of Lambda Alpha Literary Archives.
Cite As:
Douglas V. Cannon. Papers, #3014. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Land economics. Urban economics. Land use -- Economic aspects. Housing. United States. Federal Housing Administration United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration Lambda Alpha International. Golden Gate Chapter Lambda Alpha International. Los Angeles Chapter
